mnemonics: HTML Files are Pandas' Library Books: Use pd.read_html() to check them out and .to_html() to write your own.
keyPoints: pd.read_html() automatically scrapes and parses HTML tables into a list of DataFrames.
The .to_html() method converts a DataFrame into an HTML table string for embedding in a webpage.
Always specify an encoding (like 'utf-8') when reading/writing files to prevent character errors.
commonMistakes: Assuming pd.read_html() will work on any webpage without checking if the tables are built with standard <table> tags.
Forgetting that pd.read_html() returns a *list* of DataFrames, not a single DataFrame, leading to indexing errors.
Not handling file paths correctly, leading to FileNotFound errors (e.g., using a relative path when an absolute one is needed).
